context: This project trains a logistic-regression in-hospital ICU mortality classifier on MIMIC clinical notes (TF-IDF features) with patient ethnicity/race features. Mortality is rare, so the model selects on validation AUPRC; RECALL (catching actual ICU deaths) is the clinically important outcome. Data: {split}_tfidf.npz + {split}_meta.npz, meta has 'eth' (5 one-hot ethnicity/race groups) and binary mortality 'labels'. Probe for RACIAL FAIRNESS via EQUAL OPPORTUNITY: the gap in per-group recall / true-positive-rate (max group recall minus min group recall) among patients who actually died. A fair model has a small recall gap across race groups; a biased one misses deaths in some race groups more than others (overall AUROC can look fair while recall gaps are large). Report per-group recall/TPR and the gap (lower gap is better). Preserve the AUPRC utility anchor so fairness is not gamed by degrading everyone.
